questions.stem: For the applicability of Accounting Standards, COMPANIES are classified into:

options.text: Listed and unlisted only
options.explanation: Listing is one gate in the SMC definition, not the whole classification.

options.text: Two levels — MSME and Large
options.explanation: MSME/Large is the revised NON-COMPANY scheme; companies split into SMC and non-SMC.

options.text: Four levels — Level I to Level IV
options.explanation: Multi-level ladders belonged to the old NON-company classification (now itself reduced to two categories).

options.text: Two levels — Small and Medium-sized Companies (SMCs) and non-SMCs, per the Companies (AS) Rules 2021
options.explanation: The Government's criteria create exactly two company levels; non-SMCs comply with everything in full.

questions.sourceRef: SM Ch 3 §2 — two levels for companies

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT one of the conditions a company must satisfy to be an SMC?

options.text: Its equity or debt securities are not listed nor in the process of listing, in India or outside
options.explanation: The listing gate is a genuine SMC condition.

options.text: Its net worth must not exceed ₹250 crore
options.explanation: Net worth is no SMC gate at all — the financial gates are TURNOVER (≤ ₹250 crore, excluding other income) and BORROWINGS (≤ ₹50 crore).

options.text: It is not a holding or subsidiary company of a non-SMC
options.explanation: The group-ties gate is a genuine SMC condition.

options.text: It is not a bank, financial institution or insurance company
options.explanation: The excluded-business gate is a genuine SMC condition.

questions.stem: The SMC turnover condition requires turnover (excluding other income) in the immediately preceding accounting year not exceeding:

options.text: ₹100 crore
options.explanation: ₹100 crore is the Schedule III rounding threshold, not an SMC gate.

options.text: ₹250 crore
options.explanation: ₹250 crore excluding other income — with borrowings capped separately at ₹50 crore.

options.text: ₹500 crore
options.explanation: ₹500 crore is the Ind AS Phase I net-worth line — a different regime altogether.

options.text: ₹50 crore
options.explanation: ₹50 crore is the BORROWINGS ceiling, not the turnover gate.

questions.stem: Under the ICAI's revised classification effective for accounting periods commencing on or after 1 April 2024, NON-COMPANY entities are classified into:

options.text: Listed and unlisted entities
options.explanation: Listing is one criterion inside the MSME definition, not the classification itself.

options.text: Two categories — Micro, Small and Medium-sized Entities (MSMEs) and Large entities
options.explanation: The revised announcement replaced the old four-level scheme with MSME vs Large.

options.text: Four levels — Level I to Level IV
options.explanation: The four-level ladder is the SUPERSEDED March 2021 scheme.

options.text: SMCs and non-SMCs
options.explanation: SMC/non-SMC is the COMPANY classification.

readLink.label: Read: Non-company entities — MSME or Large →

questions.stem: A non-company entity qualifies as an MSME only if (among other conditions) its borrowings:

options.text: Were below ₹50 crore on the last day of the preceding year
options.explanation: A year-end dip does not cure a mid-year breach — the test is 'at any time'.

options.text: Were entirely from banks rather than public deposits
options.explanation: The source of borrowing is irrelevant; the amount is what is tested.

options.text: Did not exceed ₹250 crore during the preceding year
options.explanation: ₹250 crore is the TURNOVER gate; borrowings cap at ₹50 crore.

options.text: Did not exceed ₹50 crore AT ANY TIME during the immediately preceding accounting year
options.explanation: The borrowing test is a peak test over the whole preceding year, not a year-end snapshot.

readLink.label: Read: Non-company entities — MSME or Large →

questions.stem: Whether a non-company entity qualifies as an MSME is determined:

options.text: As at the END of the relevant accounting period
options.explanation: The Explanation to the definition fixes the assessment date at the period end.

options.text: As at the beginning of the relevant accounting period
options.explanation: The Explanation says the END of the period, not the start.

options.text: On the date the financial statements are approved
options.explanation: Approval dates have no role in the classification test.

options.text: By averaging the position over the year
options.explanation: No averaging exists — conditions are tested as at the period end (with the borrowings gate looking at any time in the preceding year).

readLink.label: Read: Non-company entities — MSME or Large →

questions.sourceRef: SM Ch 3 §2.1 — Explanation to MSME definition

questions.stem: Which set of Accounting Standards is NOT applicable to ANY MSME in its entirety?

options.text: AS 1, AS 2 and AS 10
options.explanation: Policies, inventories and PPE apply to every entity that has them.

options.text: AS 15, AS 19 and AS 29
options.explanation: These carry RELAXATIONS for MSMEs, not total exemption.

options.text: AS 3, AS 17, AS 20 and AS 24
options.explanation: Cash flow, segments, EPS and discontinuing operations are switched off for every MSME.

options.text: AS 18, AS 28 and AS 22
options.explanation: AS 18/28 switch off only for the SMALLER sub-class; AS 22 applies to current tax for all MSMEs.

questions.stem: AS 18 (Related Party Disclosures) and AS 28 (Impairment) are not applicable IN THEIR ENTIRETY to an MSME whose:

options.text: Turnover (excluding other income) does not exceed ₹50 crore AND borrowings do not exceed ₹10 crore at any time in the preceding year (and it has no ties to an MSME outside this sub-class)
options.explanation: The smaller sub-class gets the extra exemptions; other MSMEs apply AS 18 and a relaxed AS 28.

options.text: Accounts are unaudited
options.explanation: Audit status has nothing to do with the exemption.

options.text: Partners include no body corporate
options.explanation: Partner composition is no criterion.

options.text: Turnover does not exceed ₹250 crore and borrowings ₹50 crore
options.explanation: Those are the MSME gates themselves — the AS 18/28 carve-out uses the tighter ₹50 crore/₹10 crore lines.

questions.stem: For an MSME availing the AS 22 relaxation:

options.text: No tax accounting is required at all
options.explanation: Current tax must still be recognised, measured and presented per the specified paragraphs.

options.text: Only CURRENT tax is accounted for; on first availing, the accumulated deferred tax balance is adjusted against opening revenue reserves
options.explanation: The relaxation confines AS 22 to current tax, with a one-time transfer of the old deferred balance to opening reserves.

options.text: Deferred tax is carried but not updated
options.explanation: The old balance is not frozen — it is adjusted against opening revenue reserves.

options.text: Both current and deferred tax continue as before
options.explanation: Continuing deferred tax is what the relaxation removes.

questions.stem: An MSME that avails exemptions must:

options.text: File a declaration with the Registrar of Firms
options.explanation: No registry filing is prescribed.

options.text: Disclose by a note that it is an MSME and has complied with the standards as applicable to an MSME
options.explanation: The status-and-compliance note is a standing condition of availing the relief.

options.text: Obtain prior approval of the ICAI
options.explanation: No approval mechanism exists — disclosure suffices.

options.text: Say nothing, since the exemptions apply automatically
options.explanation: Silence is not permitted — the note is mandatory.

questions.stem: An entity that was an MSME last year no longer qualifies this year. The correct course is to:

options.text: Apply the full standards from the current period, WITHOUT revising previous-period figures, disclosing both the former status and the unrevised comparatives
options.explanation: Cessation is prospective; the two disclosures replace restatement.

options.text: Continue the exemptions for one grace year
options.explanation: No grace year exists — the standards apply from the current period.

options.text: Apply the full standards retrospectively from inception
options.explanation: Nothing reaches back to inception.

options.text: Restate the comparatives on the full-standard basis
options.explanation: Restatement is expressly NOT required merely on cessation.

questions.stem: An entity that was previously NOT an MSME becomes one this year. It may avail the MSME exemptions:

options.text: After ICAI condonation
options.explanation: No condonation route exists.

options.text: Immediately, from the current period
options.explanation: Immediate relief is exactly what the two-year rule denies.

options.text: Never — once large, always large
options.explanation: The status is not permanent; it just needs two consecutive qualifying years.

options.text: Only after it has remained an MSME for TWO consecutive years
options.explanation: The two-year seasoning rule stops entities dipping in and out of the exemptions.

questions.stem: May an MSME avail exemptions under SOME standards while complying in full with others?

options.text: Only with auditor pre-approval
options.explanation: No approval regime exists — disclosure and the no-misleading proviso govern.

options.text: Yes — partial availing is permitted with disclosure of the standards involved, provided it does not mislead users
options.explanation: Pick-and-choose is allowed under the announcement's conditions.

options.text: No — exemptions are all-or-nothing
options.explanation: The announcement expressly contemplates partial availing.

options.text: Yes, and no disclosure is needed
options.explanation: Disclosure of the standards availed is a condition.

questions.stem: The only Accounting Standard NOT applicable to SMCs in its ENTIRETY is:

options.text: AS 24, Discontinuing Operations
options.explanation: AS 24 is switched off for MSMEs (non-company), not for SMCs.

options.text: AS 22, Accounting for Taxes on Income
options.explanation: The current-tax-only relaxation belongs to MSMEs; SMCs apply AS 22 in full.

options.text: AS 17, Segment Reporting
options.explanation: SMCs skip AS 17 outright; the other reliefs are paragraph-level relaxations.

options.text: AS 3, Cash Flow Statements
options.explanation: AS 3 is NOT an SMC exemption — company cash-flow relief comes only from s.2(40) (OPC/small/dormant).

questions.stem: Under AS 20, an SMC is exempted from disclosing:

options.text: DILUTED earnings per share (both including and excluding extraordinary items)
options.explanation: Basic EPS stays; only the diluted figure is excused for SMCs.

options.text: Basic earnings per share
options.explanation: Basic EPS remains mandatory for SMCs.

options.text: Nothing — AS 20 applies in full
options.explanation: The diluted-EPS exemption is expressly given to SMCs.

options.text: Both basic and diluted EPS
options.explanation: Only diluted EPS is exempted — note that MSMEs (non-company) skip AS 20 altogether.

questions.stem: Under AS 28, an SMC may measure 'value in use' as:

options.text: Nil in all cases
options.explanation: Value in use is estimated, not zeroed.

options.text: A reasonable estimate, instead of computing it by the present value technique
options.explanation: Choosing the estimate route also switches off the discount-rate provisions and para 121(g) disclosure.

options.text: The asset's carrying amount
options.explanation: Using carrying amount would make the impairment test circular and meaningless.

options.text: It cannot — AS 28 applies to SMCs without relief
options.explanation: The reasonable-estimate relief is expressly available to SMCs.

questions.stem: For DEFINED BENEFIT plans, an SMC availing the AS 15 relaxations must still:

options.text: Determine the accrued liability ACTUARIALLY by the Projected Unit Credit Method, discounting by government-bond yields, and disclose the actuarial assumptions
options.explanation: SMCs escape some AS 15 paragraphs but never the PUC actuarial core — that broader escape belongs to non-company MSMEs.

options.text: Nothing — defined benefit accounting is fully exempt
options.explanation: Full exemption is not given to SMCs.

options.text: Provide on the assumption all benefits fall due at the year end
options.explanation: The 'any rational method' escape is the MSME (non-company) relief, not the SMC one.

options.text: Fund the plan through an insurer
options.explanation: Funding choice is not an AS 15 condition.

questions.stem: AS 25, Interim Financial Reporting, applies to a company:

options.text: Only to SMCs
options.explanation: If anything, it is certain NON-SMCs that regulators require to publish interim results.

options.text: Never — interim reporting is outside the AS framework
options.explanation: AS 25 exists precisely to govern interim reports when they are prepared.

options.text: Only if it is required by a regulator (e.g. SEBI quarterly results for certain non-SMCs) or elects to present an interim report
options.explanation: AS 25 itself mandates no interim report — it governs the report once one is required or chosen.

options.text: To every company, quarterly
options.explanation: No universal quarterly obligation exists in AS 25.

questions.stem: For a NON-COMPANY entity, AS 21 (Consolidated Financial Statements) applies:

options.text: Only to Large entities, compulsorily
options.explanation: Even Large entities consolidate only if required or electing.

options.text: Only if the entity is required by a regulator, or elects, to prepare consolidated financial statements
options.explanation: AS 21/23/27-CFS are conditional — they do not themselves force consolidation on non-company entities.

options.text: Never
options.explanation: It applies whenever CFS are actually required or elected.

options.text: To every entity with a subsidiary, compulsorily
options.explanation: The standard governs CFS when prepared; it does not mandate their preparation for non-company entities.

questions.stem: AS 14 (Amalgamations) and the jointly-controlled-operations/assets parts of AS 27 are, for MSMEs:

options.text: Applicable only with regulator permission
options.explanation: No permission regime exists.

options.text: Absolutely inapplicable in all circumstances
options.explanation: The carve-out yields the moment the transactions occur.

options.text: Applicable in full to every MSME
options.explanation: The annexure treats them as not applicable in the ordinary case.

options.text: Treated as not applicable because such transactions rarely occur — but the standard applies if the transactions actually exist
options.explanation: The exemption is practical, not absolute: an MSME that amalgamates applies AS 14.

questions.stem: For both the SMC and MSME turnover gates, 'turnover' means turnover:

options.text: Averaged over three years
options.explanation: No averaging exists in either definition.

options.text: EXCLUDING other income, of the immediately preceding accounting year
options.explanation: Other income is kept out of the gate on both sides of the classification.

options.text: Of the current year
options.explanation: The gate looks at the immediately PRECEDING year.

options.text: Including other income
options.explanation: Other income is expressly excluded.

readLink.label: Read: Non-company entities — MSME or Large →

questions.stem: A tiny partnership (turnover ₹2 crore, no borrowings) is a subsidiary of a LARGE entity. For AS purposes the partnership is:

options.text: An MSME for some standards only
options.explanation: Classification is entity-wide, not standard-by-standard.

options.text: An MSME on its own numbers
options.explanation: Its own numbers cannot save it from the group-ties exclusion.

options.text: NOT an MSME — a holding or subsidiary of a non-MSME is excluded whatever its own size
options.explanation: The group-ties gate overrides individual smallness.

options.text: Outside the classification entirely
options.explanation: It is classified — as a Large entity by exclusion from MSME.

readLink.label: Read: Non-company entities — MSME or Large →

questions.stem: A non-company entity that its regulator requires to follow Ind AS is, for the ICAI's MSME/Large announcement:

options.text: Outside the announcement — it follows the framework its regulator prescribes
options.explanation: The announcement expressly does not cover entities routed to Ind AS (or specific AS) by regulatory requirements.

options.text: An MSME if it meets the five gates
options.explanation: Meeting the gates is irrelevant once a regulator prescribes the framework.

options.text: Free to choose between AS and Ind AS
options.explanation: The regulator's prescription removes the choice.

options.text: A Large entity by default
options.explanation: It is not classified at all under this announcement.

readLink.label: Read: Non-company entities — MSME or Large →

questions.stem: A LARGE non-company entity must comply with:

options.text: Ind AS compulsorily
options.explanation: Ind AS applies to non-companies only where a regulator so requires.

options.text: All Accounting Standards in full
options.explanation: Large = not an MSME = no exemptions or relaxations at all.

options.text: Only the standards it considers relevant
options.explanation: Self-selection is not a compliance basis.

options.text: All standards except AS 3 and AS 17
options.explanation: Those switch-offs belong to MSMEs.

readLink.label: Read: Non-company entities — MSME or Large →

questions.stem: Regarding cash flow statements, an SMC (which is not an OPC, small or dormant company):

options.text: Prepares one every alternate year
options.explanation: No alternate-year regime exists.

options.text: Must still prepare one — AS 3 relief is NOT among the SMC exemptions; company-side relief comes only from s.2(40)
options.explanation: The SMC list starts and ends at AS 17 for total exemption; cash flow relief is a s.2(40) matter.

options.text: Is exempt from AS 3 like a non-company MSME
options.explanation: AS 3 exemption belongs to non-company MSMEs — the SMC list does not include it.

options.text: Prepares one only if listed
options.explanation: An SMC is by definition unlisted; yet it still prepares the statement.

questions.stem: The revised MSME/Large announcement for non-company entities:

options.text: Supersedes the ICAI's March 2021 announcement (the four-level classification), effective for periods commencing on or after 1 April 2024
options.explanation: The Council (August 2024) replaced the old Level I-IV ladder with the two-category scheme.

options.text: Operates alongside the four-level classification
options.explanation: The old scheme is superseded, not parallel.

options.text: Was issued by the Central Government under s.133
options.explanation: For NON-company entities the ICAI issues the criteria; s.133 notification is the company route.

options.text: Applies only to entities formed after April 2024
options.explanation: It applies to accounting PERIODS from that date, whatever the entity's age.

readLink.label: Read: Non-company entities — MSME or Large →

questions.topic: Ch 3 · Applicability · Case: sorting the Sharavati group
questions.case: Classify each non-company entity for accounting periods commencing 1 April 2024 or later: (1) Sharavati Traders, a partnership — unlisted, turnover ₹240 crore (excluding other income) last year, peak borrowings ₹45 crore, no group ties; (2) Netravati Logistics LLP — unlisted, turnover ₹260 crore last year, borrowings ₹20 crore; (3) Aghanashini Mills, a firm with turnover ₹8 crore — but it is a subsidiary of Netravati Logistics LLP; (4) Sharavati Souharda Credit Co-operative, which carries on banking business with turnover ₹30 crore.

questions.stem: Sharavati Traders (turnover ₹240 crore, peak borrowings ₹45 crore, unlisted, no ties) is:

options.text: A Large entity, because turnover exceeds ₹100 crore
options.explanation: ₹100 crore is no gate here — the turnover line is ₹250 crore.

options.text: An MSME — it clears all five gates
options.explanation: Unlisted, not a bank, turnover ≤ 250 crore, borrowings ≤ 50 crore, no non-MSME group ties.

options.text: Outside classification as a partnership
options.explanation: Partnerships are exactly the non-company entities the scheme classifies.

options.text: An SMC
options.explanation: SMC is a COMPANY category; a partnership takes the MSME/Large track.

questions.stem: Netravati Logistics LLP (turnover ₹260 crore) is:

options.text: An SMC
options.explanation: SMC is the company track; an LLP is a non-company entity.

options.text: An MSME, since its borrowings are only ₹20 crore
options.explanation: Clearing one gate cannot repair the failed turnover gate — ALL five must pass.

options.text: An MSME with partial exemptions
options.explanation: There is no in-between category.

options.text: A Large entity — the ₹250 crore turnover gate is breached, so full compliance with all AS
options.explanation: One failed gate is enough; Large entities take every standard in full.

questions.stem: Aghanashini Mills (turnover ₹8 crore, subsidiary of the Large LLP) is:

options.text: An MSME on its own tiny numbers
options.explanation: The group-ties gate defeats its individual smallness.

options.text: NOT an MSME — as a subsidiary of a non-MSME it is excluded regardless of its own size
options.explanation: Criterion (v) removes holding/subsidiaries of non-MSMEs; the ₹8 crore turnover cannot save it.

options.text: Exempt from all AS as a micro-entity
options.explanation: No blanket micro-exemption exists — it must comply as a Large-classified entity.

options.text: An MSME for disclosure standards only
options.explanation: Classification is all-or-nothing per entity.

questions.stem: Sharavati Souharda Credit Co-operative (banking business, turnover ₹30 crore) is:

options.text: An SMC
options.explanation: It is neither a company nor eligible — banks are excluded on both tracks.

options.text: An MSME because turnover is below ₹250 crore
options.explanation: The business-type gate fails first — turnover never gets tested.

options.text: An MSME if the RBI consents
options.explanation: No consent route exists; the exclusion is absolute.

options.text: NOT an MSME — banks, financial institutions and insurers are excluded whatever their size
options.explanation: Criterion (ii) excludes it despite the small turnover.

questions.topic: Ch 3 · Applicability · Case: Kabini Agencies — which exemptions exactly?
questions.case: Kabini Agencies, a partnership, qualifies as an MSME (and has been one for several years). Its figures for the immediately preceding year: turnover ₹40 crore (excluding other income), peak borrowings ₹8 crore; it is not a holding or subsidiary of any entity. Its partners ask which standards it may skip, what happens under AS 22, and what would change if turnover grew to ₹200 crore.

questions.stem: At turnover ₹40 crore and borrowings ₹8 crore, Kabini may treat as ENTIRELY inapplicable:

options.text: Every disclosure standard
options.explanation: No such blanket category exists — the list is specific.

options.text: AS 15 and AS 22 in their entirety
options.explanation: AS 15 and AS 22 carry RELAXATIONS, not total exemption.

options.text: AS 3, AS 17, AS 20, AS 24 — and additionally AS 18 and AS 28, since it sits in the ≤₹50 crore/≤₹10 crore sub-class
options.explanation: The four all-MSME switch-offs plus the small-sub-class exemption for related parties and impairment.

options.text: Only AS 3 and AS 17
options.explanation: AS 20 and AS 24 are also off for every MSME, and this entity's size adds AS 18/28.

questions.stem: If Kabini's turnover grows to ₹200 crore (borrowings unchanged), then:

options.text: Nothing changes at all
options.explanation: The sub-class exemptions (AS 18/28 in entirety) are lost.

options.text: It must adopt Ind AS
options.explanation: Ind AS has no role for a firm absent a regulator's direction.

options.text: It becomes a Large entity
options.explanation: ₹200 crore is still under the ₹250 crore MSME gate.

options.text: It remains an MSME, but AS 18 applies and AS 28 applies with relaxations — the ≤₹50 crore sub-class benefits fall away
options.explanation: MSME status survives to ₹250 crore, but the extra AS 18/28 exemption is lost above ₹50 crore.

questions.stem: Availing the AS 22 relaxation for the first time, Kabini's accumulated deferred tax liability is:

options.text: Adjusted against opening revenue reserves / owners' funds
options.explanation: The one-time transitional adjustment clears the old deferred balance to opening reserves; only current tax is accounted for thereafter.

options.text: Released to the profit and loss account
options.explanation: The transition bypasses P&L — it goes to opening reserves.

options.text: Carried frozen until reversal
options.explanation: The balance is not frozen; it is adjusted out on transition.

options.text: Ignored without any entry
options.explanation: An entry is required — against opening revenue reserves.

questions.stem: For its gratuity (defined benefit) liability, Kabini as an MSME may:

options.text: Use PUC actuarial valuation compulsorily
options.explanation: Compulsory PUC is the SMC position, not the MSME one.

options.text: Provide by ANY rational method — e.g. assuming all benefits fall due at the year end — instead of actuarial valuation
options.explanation: The rational-method escape is the NON-COMPANY MSME relief (SMCs, by contrast, must still use PUC actuarially).

options.text: Skip providing for gratuity altogether
options.explanation: The liability must still be provided — only the measurement method is relaxed.

options.text: Recognise gratuity only when paid
options.explanation: Pay-as-you-go contradicts accrual and the relaxation's own terms.

questions.topic: Ch 3 · Applicability · Case: Malnad Foods Ltd — the company track
questions.case: Malnad Foods Ltd is an unlisted company (no listing process underway), not a bank or insurer: turnover last year ₹180 crore (excluding other income), borrowings never exceeded ₹30 crore, and it is not a holding or subsidiary of any other company. It is not an OPC, small company or dormant company. Its CFO asks what the company may skip, and what changes if its parent-to-be — a listed conglomerate — completes an acquisition of 60% of its shares next year.

questions.stem: On the current facts, Malnad Foods Ltd is:

options.text: An SMC — unlisted, not a bank/insurer, turnover ≤ ₹250 crore, borrowings ≤ ₹50 crore, no non-SMC group ties
options.explanation: All five SMC gates clear.

options.text: A non-SMC because turnover exceeds ₹100 crore
options.explanation: The SMC turnover line is ₹250 crore.

options.text: An MSME
options.explanation: MSME is the NON-company category; a company is tested as SMC/non-SMC.

options.text: Ind AS-bound
options.explanation: Unlisted with these numbers, it is far below the Ind AS roadmap thresholds.

questions.stem: As an SMC, Malnad Foods may legitimately omit:

options.text: Basic EPS
options.explanation: Only DILUTED EPS is excused for SMCs.

options.text: Segment reporting (AS 17) and the diluted EPS disclosure (AS 20) — but NOT the cash flow statement
options.explanation: AS 17 goes entirely, diluted EPS is excused; AS 3 stays because the company is not OPC/small/dormant.

options.text: Related party disclosures (AS 18)
options.explanation: AS 18 relief belongs to the small NON-company sub-class, not to SMCs.

options.text: The cash flow statement
options.explanation: AS 3 is not an SMC exemption — cash-flow relief is a s.2(40) matter.

questions.stem: If the listed conglomerate acquires 60% of Malnad Foods next year, the company:

options.text: Must immediately restate all prior-year accounts
options.explanation: Status change operates prospectively; no blanket restatement arises.

options.text: Ceases to be an SMC — it becomes the subsidiary of a non-SMC
options.explanation: A listed parent is a non-SMC; criterion (v) then strips SMC status whatever Malnad's own numbers.

options.text: Becomes an MSME
options.explanation: Companies never enter the MSME classification.

options.text: Remains an SMC on its own numbers
options.explanation: The group-ties gate overrides individual size.

questions.stem: For its pension defined-benefit plan while an SMC, Malnad Foods:

options.text: Need not provide for the plan at all
options.explanation: The liability must be provided; only specified paragraphs are relaxed.

options.text: May use any rational method, like a non-company MSME
options.explanation: The rational-method escape is exclusively the non-company MSME relief.

options.text: Accounts for it on a cash basis
options.explanation: Cash-basis pension accounting is nowhere permitted.

options.text: Must determine the accrued liability actuarially by the Projected Unit Credit Method with a government-bond discount rate
options.explanation: SMC relief in AS 15 never reaches the DB core — PUC valuation stays mandatory (unlike the MSME rational-method escape).

questions.stem: Define a Small and Medium-sized Company (SMC) under the Companies (Accounting Standards) Rules 2021. (5 marks)

skeleton.point: Equity or debt securities not listed nor in the process of listing on any stock exchange, in India or outside
Not a bank, financial institution or insurance company
Turnover (excluding other income) ≤ ₹250 crore in the immediately preceding accounting year
Borrowings (including public deposits) ≤ ₹50 crore at any time during the immediately preceding accounting year
Not a holding or subsidiary company of a non-SMC; all conditions must be satisfied together

questions.stem: State the revised criteria for classifying non-company entities for AS applicability, effective 1 April 2024. (5 marks)

skeleton.point: Two categories: MSMEs and Large entities; supersedes the March 2021 (four-level) announcement; effective periods commencing on/after 1 April 2024

MSME: not listed/in process (India or abroad); not a bank/FI/insurer; turnover (excl. other income) ≤ ₹250 crore preceding year; borrowings ≤ ₹50 crore at any time in preceding year; not holding/subsidiary of a non-MSME
Conditions tested as at the end of the relevant accounting period; Large entities (all others) comply in full

questions.stem: Which Accounting Standards are not applicable to MSMEs in their entirety? Distinguish the position of AS 18 and AS 28. (5 marks)

skeleton.point: Not applicable to ANY MSME: AS 3 (Cash Flow), AS 17 (Segments), AS 20 (EPS), AS 24 (Discontinuing Operations)
AS 18 and AS 28 not applicable in entirety only to MSMEs with turnover ≤ ₹50 crore AND borrowings ≤ ₹10 crore (same test windows), not holding/subsidiary of an MSME outside the sub-class
Other MSMEs apply AS 18 fully and AS 28 with relaxations (reasonable-estimate value in use, reduced disclosures)

questions.stem: State the additional requirements that accompany the MSME exemptions (status disclosure, change of status, partial availing). (5 marks)

skeleton.point: Availing MSME must disclose by note that it is an MSME and has complied with standards as applicable to MSMEs
On ceasing to qualify: standards apply from the current period; corresponding previous-period figures need not be revised; disclose former status and the non-revision
On becoming an MSME: no exemptions until MSME for two consecutive years
Partial availing permitted with disclosure of the standards concerned; must not mislead users

questions.stem: List the exemptions and relaxations available to SMCs under the Companies (AS) Rules 2021. (5 marks)

skeleton.point: AS 17 Segment Reporting: not applicable in its entirety

AS 15: relaxations for non-vesting short-term compensated absences, discounting, and other long-term benefits — but defined benefit liability must still be determined actuarially by PUC with government-bond discount rate
AS 19: specified lease-disclosure paragraphs not applicable; AS 20: diluted EPS disclosure exempt
AS 28: value in use may be a reasonable estimate (present-value technique not compulsory); AS 29: paras 66-67 disclosures not applicable
AS 25 applies only if interim reporting is required (e.g. SEBI for certain non-SMCs) or elected

questions.stem: Contrast the AS 15 defined-benefit relief available to an SMC with that available to a non-company MSME. (4 marks)

skeleton.point: SMC: exempt from specified recognition/measurement/disclosure paragraphs, BUT must actuarially determine the DB liability by the Projected Unit Credit Method, discount by government-bond yields, and disclose actuarial assumptions
MSME (non-company): may account for DB plans (and other long-term benefits) by ANY rational method — e.g. assuming benefits fall due at the year end — no actuarial valuation required
Lesson: the deeper measurement escape belongs to non-company MSMEs; SMCs get only paragraph-level relief

questions.stem: Explain when AS 21, AS 23, AS 27 and AS 25 apply to a non-company entity. (4 marks)

skeleton.point: AS 21/23/27 (CFS aspects) do not force any non-company entity to prepare consolidated financial statements; they apply only if the entity is required (by a regulator) or elects to prepare CFS
AS 25 similarly governs interim reports only where required or elected
AS 14 and JCO/JCA parts of AS 27: treated as not applicable to MSMEs unless the transactions actually occur

questions.stem: How are companies classified for AS applicability, and what obligations attach to each class? (4 marks)

skeleton.point: Two levels under the Government's criteria: SMCs (per the AS Rules 2021 definition) and non-SMCs
Non-SMCs: comply with ALL Accounting Standards in their entirety
SMCs: exempt from AS 17; relaxations in AS 15/19/20/28/29; AS 25 conditional; AS 3 still applies (cash-flow relief is a s.2(40) matter)
